etymology.description: 運(運) (yùn, "to transport; fortune") refers to movement or transportation, which extends to imply activity. 動(動) (dòng, "to move; movement") reinforces the theme of motion or activity.
etymology.interpretation: 運(運) (yùn, "to transport; fortune") originally meant the act of carrying or moving something from one place to another, thus denoting activity and motion. 動(動) (dòng, "to move; movement") signifies physical movement or change in position, adding to the idea of actively engaging in physical activities. Together, the term 運動(運動) encompasses physical exercises and sports as activities involving coordinated movement and effort.

examples.chinese: 我每天早晨去公园运动(我每天早晨去公園運動)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ měitiān zǎochén qù gōngyuán yùndòng.
examples.english: I go to the park to exercise every morning.

examples.chinese: 他喜欢参加各种运动(他喜歡參加各種運動)。
examples.pinyin: Tā xǐhuān cānjiā gè zhǒng yùndòng.
examples.english: He likes to participate in various sports.

examples.chinese: 运动对健康有益(運動對健康有益)，可以让人保持活力(可以讓人保持活力)。
examples.pinyin: Yùndòng duì jiànkāng yǒuyì, kěyǐ ràng rén bǎochí huólì.
examples.english: Exercise is beneficial for health and can help people stay energetic.
